element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Random encounters with weak enemies teach turn-based battle system
Information gathering from townspeople drives progression; teaches that dialogue reveals quests and secrets
Resource management and stat enhancement tied to exploration depth
Unlocked through leveling; expands tactical options in combat
Requires keys (purchased) and adequate stats; increased difficulty monsters within

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Quest briefing at start, collectible throughout game
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Distributed across multiple locations; collecting drives narrative progression

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Late game, gated by stats and equipment
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Final confrontation requires high level and optimal gear; peak difficulty

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching towns with inns to recover HP/MP
typical_tension_triggers: Venturing into unexplored areas with stronger monsters
Dungeon exploration with locked doors requiring keys
Low HP/MP with limited recovery options
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual difficulty scaling tied to distance from Tantegel Castle; tension builds through exploration risk vs. reward; relief cycles through town visits and safe recovery

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Monsters increase in difficulty radiating outward from Tantegel Castle; players can choose to explore dangerous areas early, but face consequences
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: NPCs in towns reveal hidden locations and quest markers through dialogue; no physical barriers prevent exploration except locked doors requiring purchased keys

difficulty_curve.early_game: Very accessible; weak Slimes and basic enemies in immediate castle area teach mechanics safely; gates progression through stat/equipment requirements rather than hard level caps
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate difficulty increase; monsters become dangerous if player ventures too far without proper equipment; encourages grinding and town visits for supplies
difficulty_curve.late_game: Significant spike; Dragonlord's Castle requires high level (mid-20s) and optimized equipment; final boss demands substantial preparation and resources
notable_spikes: First venture beyond Tantegel area if underleveled
Dungeon exploration without adequate HP recovery
Dragonlord's Castle approach and final battle

notable_level_patterns.description: Game world organized concentrically around Tantegel Castle. Monster difficulty increases with distance from starting point, allowing players to control difficulty curve through exploration choices
Towns serve as resource acquisition and information nodes. Players retreat to towns to recover health/magic at inns, purchase equipment, and gather NPC dialogue to unlock progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tantegel (starting town), subsequent towns throughout overworld

notable_level_patterns.description: Dungeons require purchased keys and adequate stats. Each dungeon contains Balls of Light and treasure, creating mini-objectives within larger quest structure
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple mid-game dungeons leading to final castle

notable_level_patterns.description: NPC dialogue reveals locations of dungeons, items, and quest objectives. No quest markers; players must actively gather information to know where to go next
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All towns; Tantegel Castle briefing sets primary objective
